mechanism_narrative: A sustained low federal funds rate signals aggressive monetary easing that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate crossing above 4.0 percent signals labor market deterioration and rising recession probabilities, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite UNRATE crossing above 4.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising personal savings and falling housing starts signals household deleveraging and reduced consumption demand, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of instances when RSAFS rises and HOUST falls simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in the industrial production index indicates immediate industrial slowdown and supply chain stress, triggering short-term risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances following an INDPRO month-over-month decline greater than 1.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: High consumer price index combined with a near-zero federal funds rate creates inflation expectations without real rate tightening, leading to equity volatility and negative forward returns as markets price in future tightening.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances when CPIAUCSL exceeds 285 and FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust labor demand and corporate hiring activity, supporting earnings growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS growth versus SPX 60-day returns falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth rate falling below 1.0 percent signals severe demand contraction and economic stagnation, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as earnings forecasts are downgraded.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of instances when GDPC1 quarterly growth falls below 1.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous decline in industrial production and rise in the unemployment rate confirms broad-based economic weakening, triggering negative S&P 500 returns as investors rotate to safe havens.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances when both INDPRO declines and UNRATE rises simultaneously.
